subject: Sociology
title: International Organizations
description: A quick, practical guide to International Organizations.

hook.text: The United Nations has 193 member states. It can debate anything. It can enforce almost nothing.

buildup.text: International organizations like the UN, WHO, and WTO govern issues that cross borders: trade, health, security.

discovery.text: They set rules, provide aid, and mediate conflicts. But they depend on powerful states for funding and enforcement.

twist.text: Critics say these bodies serve wealthy nations' interests. The Security Council's veto power proves the point.

climax.text: International organizations represent global governance's promise and its limits — cooperation shaped by power.

quiz.question: What limits international organizations' effectiveness?
options: They depend on powerful states for funding and enforcement
